questions.question: Which institute developed and manufactured the Td vaccine?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C) Central Research Institute (CRI), Kasauli The Central Research Institute (CRI), Kasauli is the government-run institute that fully developed, tested, obtained regulatory approvals for, and started manufacturing India's indigenously produced Tetanus and Adult Diphtheria (Td) vaccine. This vaccine was officially launched there on February 21, 2026, by Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda. It marks a step toward self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in vaccine production. The CRI plans to supply 55 lakh (5.5 million) doses to the UIP by April 2026, with production scaling up further.

questions.question: With reference to the 9th IONS Conclave (2026), consider the following statements: • India assumed the Chairmanship of IONS after a gap of 16 years. • The Chairmanship was taken over from the Royal Thai Navy. • The tenure of Chairmanship is for three years (2026–2029). Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) 1 and 2 only Statement 1: Correct India assumed the Chairmanship at the 9th Conclave held in Visakhapatnam (Feb 2026) after 16 years. India had earlier chaired IONS during its initial phase after launching it in 2008. Now it has again taken leadership for 2026–2028. Statement 2: Correct India took over the Chairmanship from the Royal Thai Navy, which was the previous Chair. Chairmanship of IONS rotates among member navies. Statement 3: Incorrect The tenure of IONS Chairmanship is 2 years, not 3 years. India’s term is 2026–2028, not 2026–2029. Additional information: IONS- The Indian Ocean Naval Symposium (IONS) is a voluntary multilateral forum established by the Indian Navy in 2008. It brings together navies of the Indian Ocean littoral states for structured dialogue on regional maritime challenges, including security, humanitarian response, and cooperative operations. The conclave was part of a larger maritime engagement week in Visakhapatnam that also included Exercise MILAN 2026 and the International Fleet Review 2026, reinforcing cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region. Strategic Initiatives Announced India outlined its plans while leading IONS, such as: IONS Maritime Exercise (IMEX) to strengthen joint training. Continued deployment of IOS SAGAR for regional maritime support missions. Structured Maritime Information Sharing events to boost cooperation among navies. Admiral Dinesh K. Tripathi, Chief of the Naval Staff, took over as Chair for the 2026–2028 term.

questions.question: Arrange the following European powers in the correct chronological order of their arrival in India: • Danish • Portuguese • French • Dutch • English Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Answer: A) 2 – 4 – 5 – 1 – 3 Additional information:- Portuguese First European power to reach India: 1498 Led by Vasco da Gama (landed at Calicut) First Governor: Francisco de Almeida (Blue Water Policy) Famous Governor: Afonso de Albuquerque (captured Goa, 1510) First to introduce Cartaz system (naval pass) Capital in India: Goa Lost importance after defeat by English & Dutch Dutch (VOC – 1602) First factory: Masulipatnam (1605) Main interest: Spice trade Defeated Portuguese in many places Defeat in India: Battle of Bedara (1759) by English Strong in Indonesia, weak in Indian politics English (East India Company – 1600) Arrived: 1608 (Surat) Captain William Hawkins visited court of Jahangir First Carnatic War (1746–48) against French Major turning point: Battle of Plassey (1757) Battle of Buxar (1764) Established political control over India Danish Arrived: 1620 Settlement: Tranquebar (Tamil Nadu) Sold settlements to British in 1845 French French East India Company: 1664 Leader in India: Joseph François Dupleix Main centres: Pondicherry, Chandernagore, Mahe, Karaikal Lost to British after Carnatic Wars Important facts:- First European in India → Portuguese First battle between European powers in India → Battle of Swally (1612) (English vs Portuguese) Dutch defeated by English → 1759 (Bedara) Real political control began after → Battle of Buxar (1764) French challenge ended after → Third Carnatic War (1763 Treaty of Paris)

questions.question: The term "pleiotropy" refers to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: A) One gene affects multiple traits Pleiotropy is a genetic phenomenon where a single gene influences or controls multiple, seemingly unrelated phenotypic traits (characteristics) in an organism. Examples of Pleiotropy: In humans: The gene responsible for sickle cell anemia (a mutation in the hemoglobin beta gene) affects not just red blood cell shape (sickle-shaped cells), but also causes multiple effects like anemia, pain crises, organ damage, increased infection risk, and jaundice — all from one gene mutation. In fruit flies (Drosophila): The gene for vestigial wings not only reduces wing size but also affects other traits like haltere (balancing organ) development and flight muscles.

questions.question: Which of the following words in the Preamble were part of the Constitution as originally enacted in 1950? • Sovereign • Socialist • Secular • Democratic • Republic Select the correct answer:
questions.explanation: Answer: B) 1, 4 and 5 only • Sovereign Present in the original Preamble (1950). It means India is independent in internal and external affairs. • Socialist Not part of the original Constitution. Added later by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976 during Emergency. • Secular Also added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976. • Democratic Present in the original Preamble (1950). Indicates political democracy — government elected by the people. • Republic Present in the original Constitution. Means the head of the State is elected, not hereditary. Additional information: Originally (1950), the Preamble read: “Sovereign Democratic Republic” After the 42nd Amendment (1976), it became: “Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic” Also, “Unity of the Nation” was changed to “Unity and Integrity of the Nation”.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the nature of political authority in the Indus Valley Civilization?
options: Centralized empire under a single ruler
No clear evidence of kingship or monumental palaces
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: D) No clear evidence of kingship or monumental palaces The Indus Valley Civilization (also known as Harappan Civilization, c. 2600–1900 BCE) is unique among ancient civilizations because archaeological evidence does not show clear signs of a centralized monarchy, powerful kings, or the kinds of grand structures typically associated with royal authority (like in ancient Egypt or Mesopotamia). No monumental palaces — Excavations have revealed well-planned cities with advanced drainage, grid layouts, public baths (like the Great Bath), granaries, and large warehouses, but no structures identified as royal palaces or lavish royal residences. The largest buildings appear to be public or civic (e.g., for storage or community use), not elite royal ones. No clear evidence of kingship — There are no royal tombs, large statues of rulers in grand poses, inscriptions naming kings, stelae, or other markers of hereditary monarchy. Unlike Egypt (pharaohs' pyramids) or Mesopotamia (palaces and ziggurats), the Indus sites lack symbols of individual ruler glorification. Absence of warfare indicators — Very few weapons, no fortifications for defense against large-scale attacks, and no depictions of battles or conquests, suggesting a relatively peaceful society without a militaristic ruling class.

questions.question: Match the following List I (Local Wind) List II (Region) A. Loo 1.Switzerland B.Chinook 2. North India C. Foehn 3. Argentina D.Pampero 4. Rocky Mountains Select the correct answer:
questions.explanation: Answer: A) A-2, B-4, C-1, D-3 A. Loo → North India (2) Loo is a hot, dry summer wind. Blows over the Indo-Gangetic Plains during May–June. Causes heat waves in Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan. Hence, A-2 is correct. B. Chinook → Rocky Mountains (4) Chinook is a warm, dry downslope wind. Blows on the eastern slopes of the Rocky Mountains (USA & Canada). Known as “Snow Eater” because it melts snow rapidly. Hence, B-4 is correct. C. Foehn → Switzerland (1) Foehn (Föhn) is a warm, dry wind on the leeward side of the Alps. Common in Switzerland and nearby Alpine regions. Similar to Chinook but in Europe. Hence, C-1 is correct. D. Pampero → Argentina (3) Pampero is a cold wind. Blows across the Pampas grasslands of Argentina in South America. Associated with cold fronts. Hence, D-3 is correct.

questions.question: With reference to Cyclone Gezani, consider the following statements: • It was part of the 2025–26 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season. • It first made landfall on the eastern coast of Madagascar. • It was named by the India Meteorological Department (IMD). Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Answer: A) 1 and 2 only Statement 1: Correct Cyclone Gezani formed during the 2025–26 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season. This basin covers cyclones forming near Madagascar, Mozambique, and surrounding waters. Statement 2: Correct Gezani made its first landfall on the eastern coast of Madagascar, causing heavy rainfall, strong winds, flooding, and displacement. Statement 3: Incorrect Cyclone Gezani was not named by the India Meteorological Department (IMD). Additional information:- Cyclone naming authority depends on the ocean basin. In the North Indian Ocean (Bay of Bengal & Arabian Sea) → Names are assigned by IMD (India). In the South-West Indian Ocean → Naming is done by regional bodies under the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) framework (e.g., Meteo-France La Réunion).

questions.question: Which of the following institutions jointly developed the Global Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: C) UNDP and OPHI The Global Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) was jointly developed by: United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative (OPHI) First introduced in Human Development Report 2010 Replaced Human Poverty Index (HPI) Purpose Measures acute multidimensional poverty Goes beyond income to capture overlapping deprivations Focuses on household-level deprivation Total: 3 Dimensions, 10 Indicators Dimensions – A) Health (Nutrition, Child Mortality) B) Education (Years of Schooling, School Attendance) C) Standard of Living(Cooking Fuel, Sanitation, Drinking Water, Electricity, Housing, Assets)

questions.question: Assertion (A): The Mandal judgment introduced the concept of Creamy Layer for Other Backward Classes. Reason (R): The Court held that socially advanced members of backward classes should not avail reservation benefits.
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is incorrect
A is incorrect but R is correct
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A Assertion (A): The Mandal judgment introduced the concept of Creamy Layer for Other Backward Classes. Correct. The Supreme Court introduced the Creamy Layer doctrine to exclude the socially and educationally advanced members of OBCs from reservation benefits. Reason (R): The Court held that socially advanced members of backward classes should not avail reservation benefits. Correct. The Court reasoned that reservation is meant for the truly backward sections. If advanced members continue to take benefits, it would defeat the purpose of achieving substantive equality under Article 16(4) The Reason directly explains why the Creamy Layer principle was introduced — to prevent the more advanced sections within OBCs from monopolizing reservation benefits. Additional information:- Background of the Case Based on recommendations of the Mandal Commission (1979) headed by B.P. Mandal. Commission recommended 27% reservation for OBCs in Central services. In 1990, V.P. Singh government implemented it. The decision was challenged in Supreme Court → 9-judge bench. Key Constitutional Articles Involved Article 14 – Equality before law Article 16(1) – Equality of opportunity in public employment Article 16(4) – Reservation for backward classes

questions.question: Which ISRO mission first discovered lunar hydration?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) Chandrayaan-1 Chandrayaan-1 Launched in 2008 by ISRO. Carried NASA’s Moon Mineralogy Mapper (M³) instrument. In 2009, it confirmed the presence of water/hydroxyl molecules on the lunar surface. This was a major scientific breakthrough globally. Additional information: Chandrayaan-2 – Focused on orbiter, lander (Vikram), and rover; did not make the first hydration discovery. Chandrayaan-3 – Successfully demonstrated soft landing near the south pole. Chandrayaan-4 – Not yet an officially executed mission.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Mumbai's architectural heritage is/are correct? 1. Victorian Gothic buildings in Mumbai reflect colonial-era architecture with Neo-Gothic elements. 2. Art Deco buildings in Mumbai were part of the 19th-century colonial urban development. 3. The Oval Maidan distinctly separates the Victorian Gothic and Art Deco architectural styles.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: C) Only 1 and 3 are correct 1. Victorian Gothic buildings in Mumbai reflect colonial-era architecture with Neo-Gothic elements. Correct Built mainly in the late 19th century during British rule. Inspired by Neo-Gothic architecture of Europe. Examples include: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus University of Mumbai 2. Art Deco buildings in Mumbai were part of the 19th-century colonial urban development. Incorrect Mumbai’s Art Deco buildings were constructed mainly in the 1930s–1940s (20th century). They reflect modernist, streamlined designs—not 19th-century colonial development. 3. The Oval Maidan distinctly separates the Victorian Gothic and Art Deco architectural styles. Correct The Oval Maidan acts as a spatial divider: One side → Victorian Gothic buildings Opposite side → Art Deco buildings This unique juxtaposition led to UNESCO recognition in 2018.

questions.question: With reference to Mahmud of Ghazni, consider the following statements: 1. He invaded India primarily to establish a permanent empire. 2. His most famous raid was on the Somnath temple. 3. He defeated Jayapala of the Hindu Shahi dynasty. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) 2 and 3 only 1. He invaded India primarily to establish a permanent empire. Incorrect Mahmud’s Indian campaigns (17 raids between 1000–1027 CE) were mainly for: Wealth acquisition Political prestige Religious legitimacy He did not attempt to build a long-term territorial empire in North India. His base remained in Ghazni (present-day Afghanistan). 2. His most famous raid was on the Somnath temple. Correct In 1025 CE, he attacked the Somnath Temple (Gujarat). This raid became historically famous due to: Immense loot Symbolic religious significance 3. He defeated Jayapala of the Hindu Shahi dynasty. Correct Jayapala, ruler of the Hindu Shahi dynasty, was defeated by Mahmud in early campaigns. Later, Anandapala (Jayapala’s successor) also faced him.

questions.question: What is the name of the festival celebrated simultaneously by unmarried tribal girls during the Karam celebrations in Jharkhand and eastern India?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) Jawa During the Karam (Karma) festival, unmarried tribal girls celebrate a special ritual known as Jawa. The Karam festival is widely celebrated in Jharkhand and parts of eastern India by tribal communities such as the Oraon, Munda, and Santhal. Jawa Ritual performed by unmarried girls. They sow jawa (barley or grain seeds) in baskets several days before Karam. The sprouted grains are worshipped during the festival. Additional information:- Sohrai A harvest and cattle festival celebrated mainly after Diwali in Jharkhand. Karma Refers to the broader Karam festival itself, not the specific ritual by unmarried girls. Madai A tribal festival mainly celebrated in Chhattisgarh. Festivals of Jharkhand Karam, Sarhul, Sohrai, Mage Parab, Tusu Parab, Jani Shikar,Baa Parab, Hal Punhya, Bandna Folk Dances of Jharkhand Chhau,Paika, Jhumair (Jhumar), Domkach, Fagua, Santhali Dance, Mundari Dance, Karma Dance, Natua

questions.question: What important event is celebrated during the festival of 'Baisakhi' in Chandigarh?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: D) Founding of Khalsa Panth During the festival of Baisakhi, especially in places like Chandigarh and across Punjab, Sikhs commemorate the founding of the Khalsa Panth. In 1699, the tenth Sikh Guru, Guru Gobind Singh, formally established the Khalsa at Anandpur Sahib. This event marked the creation of a distinct Sikh identity with the introduction of the Five Ks and the initiation ceremony (Amrit Sanskar). Additional information: Guru Nanak – His birth anniversary is celebrated as Guru Nanak Gurpurab, not Baisakhi. Golden Temple – Its foundation was laid in 1581 by Guru Ram Das. Guru Angad – His birth anniversary is celebrated separately as a Gurpurab. important facts from Sikh history Foundation of Sikhism Sikhism was founded by Guru Nanak in the late 15th century. Born in 1469 at Talwandi (now Nankana Sahib, Pakistan). Emphasized: Naam Japna, Kirat Karna, Vand Chhakna. Compilation of Adi Granth (1604) The Adi Granth was compiled by Guru Arjan. Installed at Golden Temple. Later became Guru Granth Sahib (eternal Guru of Sikhs).

questions.question: For a concave mirror, when the object distance is equal to the radius of curvature, the image produced will be:
options: Real, inverted, and equal in size
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) Real, inverted, and equal in size In a concave mirror: The radius of curvature (R) = 2 × focal length (f). The center of curvature (C) lies at distance R from the pole. If the object is placed at C (i.e., object distance = radius of curvature), then: Image Characteristics: Position: At C Nature: Real (rays actually meet) Orientation: Inverted Size: Same as object Magnification (m): –1 Negative → inverted Magnitude 1 → same size

questions.question: The spectacular light displays seen in high-latitude regions, commonly called auroras, are produced due to:
options: Seismic disturbance beneath the Earth’s crust
Interaction between solar charged particles and Earth’s magnetic field
Refraction of sunlight in polar ice crystals
Volcanic emissions in the upper atmosphere
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (B) Interaction between solar charged particles and Earth’s magnetic field Auroras occur near the polar regions due to interaction between solar wind (charged particles from the Sun) and the Earth’s magnetosphere. These charged particles collide with gases like oxygen and nitrogen in the upper atmosphere. This collision releases energy in the form of light. They are known as: Aurora Borealis (Northern Hemisphere) Aurora Australis (Southern Hemisphere) Other Options: (a) Seismic disturbances → Related to earthquakes, not auroras. (c) Refraction in ice crystals → Causes halo phenomena, not auroras. (d) Volcanic emissions → Do not create polar light displays.

questions.question: Consider the following statements on the Green Hydrogen Mission: • It aims to make India a global hub for production and export of green hydrogen. • The mission targets production of 10 MMT by 2030. • Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis using renewable energy.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Only 1 and 3 are correct. Statement 1: Correct The mission aims to make India a global hub for production, usage, and export of green hydrogen. Statement 2: Incorrect The mission targets 5 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production per annum by 2030, not 10 MMT. Statement 3: Correct Green hydrogen is produced through electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power. Additional information: Launched in 2023 Target: 5 MMT per year by 2030 Expected renewable capacity addition: ~125 GW Focus sectors: Fertilizers, Refineries, Steel, Export market Supports India’s net-zero target (2070)

questions.question: At the 1960 Rome Olympics, Milkha Singh set an Indian record by finishing Fourth in the men’s 400 meters with a timing of ______________, a record that stood until 1984.
questions.explanation: At the 1960 Summer Olympics, Milkha Singh competed in the men’s 400 metres final. Event: 400 metres (Athletics) Venue: Rome, Italy Position: 4th place Timing: 45.6 seconds (hand-timed) Electronic timing recorded as 45.73 seconds Significance: Set a new Indian national record This record remained unbroken until 1984 Lost the bronze medal by a very narrow margin (about 0.1 second)

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding change of state: • During melting, temperature remains constant until the solid completely changes into liquid. • Latent heat is the heat required to change the state of matter without change in temperature. • Boiling occurs only at the surface of a liquid. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) 1 and 2 only Statement 1 – Correct During melting, the temperature does not increase even though heat is supplied. The supplied heat is used to overcome intermolecular forces. This heat is called latent heat of fusion. Example: Ice at 0°C remains at 0°C until fully converted into water. Statement 2 – Correct Latent heat is the energy absorbed or released during a change of state without change in temperature. Two types: Latent heat of fusion (solid → liquid) Latent heat of vaporization (liquid → gas) Statement 3 – Incorrect Boiling does not occur only at the surface. Boiling occurs throughout the liquid (bulk phenomenon). Surface-only vaporization is called evaporation. Hence this statement is wrong. Additional information: Evaporation → Surface phenomenon (any temperature) Boiling → Bulk phenomenon (fixed boiling point)

questions.question: The 16th edition of Exercise Vajra Prahar was held in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: B) Himachal Pradesh The 16th edition of Exercise Vajra Prahar was conducted at the Special Forces Training School, Bakloh, located in Himachal Pradesh. The exercise aims to: Deepen defence cooperation and mutual understanding. Enhance operational interoperability between Indian and US special forces. Exchange advanced tactics, techniques and procedures in special operations. Build greater mutual trust and professional camaraderie. The 16th edition began on 24 February 2026 at the Special Forces Training School, Bakloh in Himachal Pradesh. It will continue for about three weeks, until mid-March.

questions.question: The integration of the princely states into the Indian Union after 1947 involved a complex interplay of political negotiation, popular movements, and, at times, coercion. Which of the following factors most significantly undermined the initial aspirations of some princely rulers to maintain independent or autonomous status?
options: The overwhelming military superiority of the newly formed Pakistani state, compelling the rulers to seek protection within India.
The consistent and unwavering support provided by the British government to the princely states seeking independence.
The strong undercurrent of popular will within the princely states favoring accession to India, often expressed through organized movements and protests.
The economic prosperity and stability offered by remaining independent, making integration into the financially strained Indian Union unattractive.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: C) The strong undercurrent of popular will within the princely states favoring accession to India, often expressed through organized movements and protests. After Independence in 1947, around 565 princely states had the option to join India or Pakistan, or theoretically remain independent. Under the leadership of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel and the diplomatic strategy of V. P. Menon, most princely states acceded to India. However, what significantly undermined the ambitions of rulers who wanted independence (e.g., Hyderabad, Junagadh, Kashmir initially) was: Strong Popular Pressure Many princely states had active Praja Mandal movements. The people demanded democratic governance and integration with India. Rulers lacked broad-based legitimacy to sustain independence. Internal unrest made continued autonomy politically unstable. Thus, popular will combined with political negotiation was a decisive factor.

questions.question: With reference to cropping seasons in India, consider the following statements: • Rabi crops are sown after the withdrawal of the Southwest Monsoon. • Kharif crops require high temperature and high humidity. • Zaid crops are grown during the short season between Rabi and Kharif. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: C) 1, 2 and 3 Statement 1: Rabi crops are sown after the withdrawal of the Southwest Monsoon. Correct Southwest Monsoon withdraws around October. Rabi crops are sown in October–November. They are harvested in March–April. They depend mainly on: Residual soil moisture Western Disturbances (winter rainfall in North India) Examples: Wheat, Barley, Mustard, Gram Statement 2: Kharif crops require high temperature and high humidity. Correct Kharif crops are sown with the onset of Southwest Monsoon (June–July). They require: High temperature High rainfall High humidity Examples: Rice, Maize, Cotton, Sugarcane Statement 3: Zaid crops are grown during the short season between Rabi and Kharif. Correct Zaid season: March to June It is the summer cropping season. These crops require irrigation. Examples: Watermelon, Muskmelon, Cucumber, Fodder crops

questions.question: Which of the following tissues is dead at maturity? • Parenchyma • Sclerenchyma • Xylem vessels • Collenchyma Select the correct answer:
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: B) 2 and 3 only Plant tissues are mainly classified into two types — Living tissues Dead tissues 1. Parenchyma (Parenchyma): Parenchyma is a living simple permanent tissue. The cells have thin cell walls and contain cytoplasm and a nucleus. This tissue performs functions such as storage of food, photosynthesis, and gas exchange. 2. Sclerenchyma (Sclerenchyma): Sclerenchyma is a dead tissue at maturity. The cells have very thick, lignified cell walls, which provide mechanical strength and rigidity to different parts of the plant. 3. Xylem vessels (Xylem vessels): Xylem vessels are also dead at maturity. They form long, hollow tubes that transport water and mineral salts from the roots to other parts of the plant. 4. Collenchyma (Collenchyma): Collenchyma is a living tissue. The cells have unevenly thickened cell walls and provide flexibility and support to growing parts of the plant such as the stem and leaf petiole. Conclusion: Thus, the tissues that are dead at maturity are Sclerenchyma and Xylem vessels

questions.question: Read the below statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Mark the correct options: Assertion (A): The Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam recognizes electronic records as primary evidence. Reason (R): Section 62 states that electronic records are secondary unless accompanied by a printed certificate.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, R is false.
A is false, R is true.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: C) A is true, R is false. Assertion (A): True The Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, 2023 (which replaced the Indian Evidence Act, 1872) gives electronic records the same legal status as other documents. Electronic records are recognized as primary evidence, provided they are produced in original form (e.g., original device/server data). Reason (R): False The statement about Section 62 declaring electronic records as secondary unless accompanied by a certificate is incorrect. Under the old Indian Evidence Act, Section 65B required a certificate for admissibility of electronic evidence (when produced as secondary evidence). Under the new Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, electronic records are explicitly treated as documents and admissibility provisions have been streamlined. Section 62 does not state what is mentioned in the Reason. Key points:- Electronic record = Documentary evidence Certificate requirement applies when electronic record is produced as secondary evidence Original electronic record = Primary evidence Old Law → Section 65B certificate (secondary electronic evidence) New Law → Clear recognition + digital-age friendly provisions

questions.question: Rajiv purchased a commercial shop for ₹12,00,000 he wants to earn a 10% annual return on his investment after paying ₹3,500 per month for maintain. what should be the monthly the charges to achieve this?
questions.explanation: He wants annual profit of 1,20,000(10%) and his annual expense is 3500×12 = 42,000 Total annual return = 1,20,000+42,000 = 1,62,000 Hence, Monthly rent = 1,62,000/12=₹13,500

questions.question: A vertical cylindrical contains is filled with oil. Two solid spherical iron ball of radius 6cm is dropped into the jar and is completely submerged. If the radius of the cylindrical container is 8cm, by how much will oil level rise?
questions.explanation: Volume of liquid displaced = Volume of object submerges. π × 8 × 8 × h=2 × 4/3 π × 6 × 6 × 6. ⇒ h = 9cm

questions.question: A furniture retailer offers a 15% trade discount on a sofa set with a marked price of ₹1,20,000. However, during a clearance sale, the sofa is finally sold for ₹91,800 after an additional cash discount offered?
questions.explanation: After 15% discount, Price become 120000×85/100=1,02,000 Now, Cash discount = 102000-91800=10,200 Cash discount% = 10200/102000×100=10%

questions.question: A thief is spotted by a security guard from a distance of 400m. when the guard starts the chase, the thief also starts running. If the speed of thief is 10km/hr and speed of the guard is 12km/hr. what is the distance between them after 6 minutes?
questions.explanation: Relative speed difference between them is 2km/hr. Initially there is gap of 400m but in 6 minutes guard will cover relatively. 2km/hr × 6min =(2×1000)/60×6=200m Hence distance between them after 6 minutes is (400 – 200)m = 200m

questions.question: Gaurav invested Rs. ‘y’ in a scheme offering 20% compound interest compounded annually for two years. Sneha inveest ₹15,000 in a different scheme offering 12% simple interest for five years. Find the interest earned by Gaurav if the interest earned by Sneha is ₹(2y + 600).
questions.explanation: Sneha interest 2y + 600 = (15000 × 12 × 5)/100 = 9000 ⇒ 2y = 8400 ⇒ y = 4200 The same amount is interest by Gaurav A = 4200(1 + 20/100)² = 4200 × 6/5 × 6/5 = 6048 Hence interest earned = 6048 – 4200 = 1848

questions.question: What is the unit digit of product of the first 20 prime numbers?
questions.explanation: First 20 prime number – 2, 3, 5, ……. Here 2 × 5=10

questions.question: A decorative floor tile is in shape of a regular octagon. What is the internal angle between any two adjacent sides?
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = (360°)/(no.of sides) = (360°)/8=45° Hence interior angle = 180° - 45° = 135°

questions.question: Raj’s present age is two-seventh of the age of his mother. After 6 years, he will be one-third of the age of his mother. How old is the mother at present?
questions.explanation: R=2/7 m⇒7R=2m And after 6 years. R+6=1/3 (m+6) ⇒3R+18=m+6 ⇒6/7 m+18=m+6 ⇒6m+126=7m+42 ⇒6m+126=7m+42 ⇒M=84

questions.question: Find the median of the following distribution. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773402325Math%20Paid%20-%208_html_b6009599.png)
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1773402325Math%20Paid%20-%208_html_692252ab.png) Here N=40, N/2=20, 20 lies in (20-30) Class interval median = l + ((n/2 - cf)/f) × h l = lower limit of median class = 20 n/2 = 20 cf = Cumulative frequency of preceding class = 13 f = frequency of median class = 12 h = class width = (30-20) = 10 Median = 20 + (20-13)/12 × 10 = 20 + 70/12 = 25.83

questions.question: A decorative is shaped live a square based pyramid. It is designed to fix perfectly inside a cubic box with a side length of 9cm, such that the base of the pyramid covers the bottom of the box and its tip just touches the top lid. What is the volume, of glass pyramid?
questions.explanation: Since it is square based pyramid Area of base = 9×9 = 81 cm² Height of pyramid is equal to the height of cubic box which is 9 cm So, Volume = 1/3 × Area of base × Height = 1/3 × 81 × 9 = 243 cm³

questions.question: A cone, a hemisphere and a cylinder stand on equal bases and have the same height (h=r). What is the ratio of their volumes?
questions.explanation: Cone : Hemisphere : Cylinder 1/3 πr² h : 2/3 πr³ : πr² h 1/3 πr³ : 2/3 πr³ : πr³ 1 : 2 : 3

questions.question: A and B together can complete a project in 24 days if A is 25% more effecient than B, in how many days can B, working alone, complete 50% of the project?
questions.explanation: Efficiency of A∶B 5∶4 In 24 days they both will do 24×9=216 unit of work B has to do 50% of work = 108 unit Time taken by B = 108/4=27 days

questions.question: A bag contain ₹10, ₹5 and ₹2 coins in the ratio 4 : 5 : 6. if the total amount of money in the beg is ₹2310, find the number of ₹5 coins?
questions.explanation: Ratio of coins value 40 : 25 : 12 Sum of them = 2310 77x = 2310 ⇒ x=30 No. of ₹5 coins = 30×5=150

questions.question: If 2 liters of concentrated fruit juice are mixed with x liters of water. If the fruit juice makes up 4% of the final mixture, find the value of x.
questions.explanation: % of fruit juice =2/(2+x)×100=4 ⇒ 50=2+x⇒x=48l

questions.explanation: Only conclusion I follows because the mandate directly implies water scarcity as the reason. Conclusion II does not, as the requirement is limited to commercial buildings only, excluding residential ones.

questions.question: What angle will be drawn between the minute hand and hour hand in one minute?
questions.explanation: A clock is a circle of 360°. The minute hand moves 360° in 60 minutes, so 6° per minute. The hour hand moves 360° in 12 hours (720 minutes), so 0.5° per minute. In one minute, the minute hand gains 6° - 0.5° = 5.5° on the hour hand.

questions.question: On Adi Enclave, five flats are numbered 10-18, even number: M is at 16; N is at 14; K is immediately below N; O is at the top, then who lives at flat number12?
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - Replace "are" with "is". Grammatical Concept - Compound Nouns as a Unit: Even though "bread" and "butter" are two different things, when they are served together as a single meal or concept, they take a singular verb. Common Pairs: Other examples include "Rice and curry is...", "Time and tide waits...", and "Horse and carriage is...". Why this is a trap: Because the sentence is longer, your eyes might see "Fresh bread" and "butter" as separate entities. However, the phrase "a very wholesome breakfast" in the third segment confirms that they are being treated as one single unit. Correct Sentence: Fresh bread and butter is usually considered as a very wholesome breakfast.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. "The old stray dog that hangs around the tea stall is actually __________, but he still manages to run fast whenever he sees the biscuit packet."
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- lame of one leg. 📌 Explanation — Use "of" with "lame" when referring to a specific limb. Fixed Preposition: When the word "lame" (unable to walk properly) is followed by a specific part of the body (like a leg), the standard preposition used is "of". The "In" Trap: In daily conversation, people often say "lame in the leg," but for formal exams, "lame of" is the preferred idiomatic expression. Why other options are incorrect: Lame in one leg: Very common in casual English, but usually marked wrong in strict grammar tests which look for "lame of." Lame with / Lame by: These prepositions do not form a recognized idiomatic pair with "lame" to describe a disability. Correct Sentence: The old stray dog that hangs around the tea stall is actually lame of one leg, but he still manages to run fast whenever he sees the biscuit packet.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. "My grandmother gifted me a wooden, old, beautiful, brown rocking chair that has been in our family for three generations."
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- beautiful, old, brown, wooden 📌 Explanation — The adjectives must follow the OSASCOMP hierarchy. Order Category Examples 1. O Opinion beautiful, ugly, adroit, delicious, strange 2. S Size big, tiny, enormous, short, tall 3. A Age old, new, ancient, youthful, antique 4. S Shape square, round, flat, rectangular, triangular 5. C Colour brown, crimson, blue, yellowish, silver 6. O Origin Chinese, Indian, Italian, lunar, Victorian 7. M Material wooden, silk, gold, plastic, cotton 8. P Purpose rocking (chair), sleeping (bag), cleaning (tool)

questions.question: Identify the type of sentence- The adroit professor explained the complex theory clearly, yet some students found the lecture quite confusing.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (2)- Compound Sentence. 📌 Explanation - Breaking down the clauses To identify the type, we count the Independent Clauses (can stand alone) and Dependent Clauses (cannot stand alone). Clause 1: "The adroit professor explained the complex theory clearly" (Independent). Connector: "yet" (A coordinating conjunction - FANBOYS). Clause 2: "some students found the lecture quite confusing" (Independent).  Why it is a Compound Sentence: • It contains two independent clauses. • They are joined by a coordinating conjunction (yet). • There are no dependent clauses (clauses starting with because, although, who, which, etc.).  Quick Cheat Sheet for your Test: Simple: 1 Independent Clause. Compound: 2+ Independent Clauses (joined by for, and, nor, but, or, yet, so). Complex: 1 Independent + 1 or more Dependent Clauses. Compound-Complex: 2+ Independent + 1 or more Dependent Clauses.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. "The older kids in the neighbourhood used to pick at the shy boy just because he wore glasses and liked to read during recess."
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- pick on. 📌 Explanation — Replace "pick at" with "pick on." Grammatical Concept: Pick on (Phrasal Verb): This means to repeatedly treat someone unfairly, criticize them, or tease them (bullying). Pick at: This means to eat small amounts of food without appetite, or to pull at something with your fingers (like a scab or a loose thread). It doesn't fit the context of teasing. Why other options are incorrect: Pick up: To lift something, or to improve (e.g., "The weather is picking up"). Pick out: To choose or identify someone or something from a group. Correct Sentence: The older kids in the neighbourhood used to pick on the shy boy just because he wore glasses and liked to read during recess.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. "The diplomat’s reply was intentionally __________, as he wanted to avoid committing to a specific agreement before consulting with his government."
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- equivocal. 📌 Explanation — The keyword is "avoid committing." Logic & Vocabulary: Equivocal (Correct): If you want to avoid a firm commitment, you use language that is non-committal and open to interpretation. An equivocal answer allows you to "back out" later because you never said anything definitive. Dogmatic: This describes someone who expresses their opinions as if they are absolute facts. A dogmatic person is the opposite of equivocal—they are very firm and unyielding, which is the last thing this diplomat wants to be. Scathing: This means bitterly severe or hurtful. Being mean (scathing) wouldn't necessarily help the diplomat avoid a commitment; it would just start an argument. Lucid: If the diplomat were lucid (clear/easy to understand), he would be making his position very obvious, which goes against his goal of staying vague. Correct Sentence: The diplomat’s reply was intentionally equivocal, as he wanted to avoid committing to a specific agreement before consulting with his government.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation — Remove "but". Grammatical Concept: The "Although" Rule: "Although" and "Though" are subordinating conjunctions that already express a contrast. Redundancy: You cannot use both "Although" and "But" in the same sentence. It is considered a double conjunction error. The Alternative: You can use a comma alone, or you can use "yet" instead of "but," though a comma is preferred in modern exams. Correct Sentence: Although he is very wealthy, he lives a very simple and frugal life.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The traveler stayed in a remote cabin deep within the forest, miles away from the nearest neighbor.
options: The explorers ventured into a remote region of the desert that had never been mapped.
The village was so remote that it took two days of hiking just to reach the local market.
She couldn't find the television remote, so she had to get up to turn off the news manually.
Working from a remote location has become much easier with the help of high-speed internet.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - Identifying the shift in meaning: The Original Meaning: In the prompt, remote is an adjective describing a physical place that is far away or secluded. Analysing the Options: Option 1: Refers to a distant desert region. (Same Meaning) Option 2: Refers to a secluded village. (Same Meaning) Option 3: Refers to a device (remote control). This is the homonym because while the spelling is identical, it is a noun representing an object, not an adjective describing distance. Option 4: Refers to a distant or off-site work location. (Same Meaning)

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. P: Eventually, he became so adroit at the craft that people travelled from distant towns just to see his work. Q: When he was just a young boy, he started helping his father in the small workshop. R: However, it took years of dedicated practice and many failed attempts to reach level of mastery. S: A local carpenter once lived in a quiet village near the mountains.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- SQRP. 📌 Logical Steps for Arrangement: Opening (S): Introduces the subject (The carpenter). Early Life (Q): Establishes the beginning of his journey as a boy. The Struggle (R): Highlights the "years of practice" and "failed attempts." Since we removed "that," R now stands as a general description of his hard work. The Result (P): Starts with "Eventually," which signals the final outcome of the years mentioned in R. He is now "adroit" and famous.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Could the mystery not have been solved by Jaman?
options: Could Jaman not have solved the mystery?
Could Jaman have not solved the mystery?
Can Jaman not have solved the mystery?
Could the mystery not be solved by Jaman?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation - This question tests your ability to maintain the "perfect" modal structure ($modal + have + V3$) while reverting from a passive "been" structure to an active one. Tense & Modal Consistency: The passive sentence uses "could... have been solved." This is a past modal capability. To convert to active, we must keep "could... have solved." Option (C) is incorrect because it changes "could" to "can," altering the tense/possibility. Negative Placement: In a modal question, the word "not" typically follows the subject in formal writing. Option (A) ("Could Jaman not...") is the standard, clean active structure. Option (B) is less formal and puts "not" in a position that awkwardly splits the auxiliary verb phrase. Structure Reversion: In the passive version, "the mystery" was the subject. In the active version, Jaman must become the subject. Option (D) is incorrect because it keeps "the mystery" as the subject and simplifies the tense, losing the "have been" (perfect) aspect entirely. The "Been" Removal: When moving from passive to active in perfect tenses, the marker "been" must be removed. Option (A) correctly identifies that Jaman is the one doing the "solving."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. Manisha said, "I must leave for the airport immediately."
options: Manisha said that she must leave for the airport immediately.
Manisha said that she had to leave for the airport immediately.
Manisha said that she would have to leave for the airport immediately.
Manisha told that she had to leave for the airport immediately.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - This question tests your ability to handle the modal verb "must" when it represents a specific necessity in the past. The "Had to" Shift: When "must" refers to a specific, one-time obligation (like catching a flight), it backshifts to "had to" in indirect speech. Option (A) is incorrect because it fails to backshift the necessity to the past. Universal vs. Specific: If Manisha had said "We must obey the law," the "must" would stay "must" because it's a permanent rule. Since this is a specific flight, we use "had to." Tense Consistency: Option (C) uses "would have to," which implies a future necessity from a past perspective. While used in some contexts, "had to" is the standard direct conversion for a current necessity. Reporting Verb: Since there is no listener (no "me" or "her"), we keep "said." Option (D) is incorrect because "told" requires a person to be mentioned. Pronoun Change: The first-person "I" correctly changes to "she" to refer back to Manisha. Grammatical concept: Must  Had to: Used for specific obligations or immediate necessities. Must  Must: Used for universal truths, permanent rules, or moral laws (e.g., "One must do one's duty").

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Sustainable Development" has evolved from a niche environmental concern to a central pillar of global governance. At its core, it seeks to balance three often-competing dimensions: economic growth, social inclusion, and environmental protection. However, the implementation of these goals is frequently stymied by "short-termism" -the tendency of governments and corporations to prioritize immediate financial or political gains over long-term ecological health. This is particularly evident in the exploitation of fossil fuels, where the immediate energy needs of a growing population are met at the expense of climate stability. To transition toward a truly green economy, experts argue that a systemic shift is required, moving away from Gross Domestic Product (GDP) as the sole measure of success and instead incorporating "Natural Capital" into national accounting. This would involve valuing forests, clean water, and biodiversity as tangible assets, rather than inexhaustible free resources. Question: What is the primary obstacle to the implementation of sustainable development mentioned in the passage?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- Short-termism in decision-making. 📌 Explanation: The passage explicitly states that the implementation of sustainable development goals is "frequently stymied by short-termism." It defines this as the tendency to prioritize immediate gains over long-term health. Passage Insight: Barrier Identification: In RC passages, look for "transition words" like However. The passage says: "However, the implementation... is frequently stymied by 'short-termism'." This identifies the primary hurdle. Why other options are incorrect: (A) Lack of global governance: The passage says sustainable development is a pillar of global governance, not that it is lacking. (B) Economic growth and social inclusion: These are dimensions of the concept itself, not obstacles. (D) Over-reliance on Natural Capital: The author suggests we should use Natural Capital; we aren't over-reliant on it yet.

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Sustainable Development" has evolved from a niche environmental concern to a central pillar of global governance. At its core, it seeks to balance three often-competing dimensions: economic growth, social inclusion, and environmental protection. However, the implementation of these goals is frequently stymied by "short-termism" -the tendency of governments and corporations to prioritize immediate financial or political gains over long-term ecological health. This is particularly evident in the exploitation of fossil fuels, where the immediate energy needs of a growing population are met at the expense of climate stability. To transition toward a truly green economy, experts argue that a systemic shift is required, moving away from Gross Domestic Product (GDP) as the sole measure of success and instead incorporating "Natural Capital" into national accounting. This would involve valuing forests, clean water, and biodiversity as tangible assets, rather than inexhaustible free resources. Question: According to the passage, how is the exploitation of fossil fuels a reflection of "short-termism"?
options: It prioritizes immediate energy needs over ecological health.
It reduces the gap between the rich and the poor.
It focuses on environmental protection over economic growth.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- It prioritizes immediate energy needs over ecological health. 📌 Explanation: The text provides fossil fuel exploitation as an example of short-termism, noting that "immediate energy needs... are met at the expense of climate stability." Passage Insight: Cause and Effect: The passage links "immediate needs" (the cause) with the sacrifice of "climate stability/ecological health" (the effect), which fits the definition of short-termism. Why other options are incorrect: (A) Promotes climate stability: The passage says the opposite—it happens at the expense of stability. (C) Reduces the gap: This is not mentioned in relation to fossil fuels. (D) Environmental protection over growth: The passage argues the current trend is the exact opposite.

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Sustainable Development" has evolved from a niche environmental concern to a central pillar of global governance. At its core, it seeks to balance three often-competing dimensions: economic growth, social inclusion, and environmental protection. However, the implementation of these goals is frequently stymied by "short-termism" -the tendency of governments and corporations to prioritize immediate financial or political gains over long-term ecological health. This is particularly evident in the exploitation of fossil fuels, where the immediate energy needs of a growing population are met at the expense of climate stability. To transition toward a truly green economy, experts argue that a systemic shift is required, moving away from Gross Domestic Product (GDP) as the sole measure of success and instead incorporating "Natural Capital" into national accounting. This would involve valuing forests, clean water, and biodiversity as tangible assets, rather than inexhaustible free resources. Question: What systemic shift do experts suggest for a transition to a green economy?
options: Increasing the reliance on traditional GDP metrics.
Treating forests and water as inexhaustible free resources.
Incorporating "Natural Capital" into measures of success.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D)- Incorporating "Natural Capital" into measures of success. 📌 Explanation: To transition to a green economy, the passage suggests "moving away from GDP as the sole measure... and instead incorporating 'Natural Capital' into national accounting." Passage Insight: Proposed Solutions: When a passage identifies a problem (short-termism/GDP focus), look for the "expert" recommendation. Here, the recommendation is the shift toward "Natural Capital." Why other options are incorrect: (A) Increasing reliance on GDP: This is the current problem, not the solution. (B) Treating resources as inexhaustible: This is the current flawed view that the author wants to change. (C) Moving away from national accounting: The author wants to change how we account, not stop doing it entirely.

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Sustainable Development" has evolved from a niche environmental concern to a central pillar of global governance. At its core, it seeks to balance three often-competing dimensions: economic growth, social inclusion, and environmental protection. However, the implementation of these goals is frequently stymied by "short-termism" -the tendency of governments and corporations to prioritize immediate financial or political gains over long-term ecological health. This is particularly evident in the exploitation of fossil fuels, where the immediate energy needs of a growing population are met at the expense of climate stability. To transition toward a truly green economy, experts argue that a systemic shift is required, moving away from Gross Domestic Product (GDP) as the sole measure of success and instead incorporating "Natural Capital" into national accounting. This would involve valuing forests, clean water, and biodiversity as tangible assets, rather than inexhaustible free resources. Question: Which of the following can be inferred as a "tangible asset" under the proposed new accounting system?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- Biodiversity. 📌 Explanation: The final sentence states that the new system would involve valuing "forests, clean water, and biodiversity as tangible assets." Passage Insight: Contextual Vocabulary: The passage redefines "assets" to include natural elements. Biodiversity is listed directly as one of these proposed tangible assets. Why other options are incorrect: (A) & (C): These are "immediate gains" which the author views as the problem, not the long-term assets of a green economy. (D) GDP growth: The passage suggests GDP is an insufficient metric.

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Sustainable Development" has evolved from a niche environmental concern to a central pillar of global governance. At its core, it seeks to balance three often-competing dimensions: economic growth, social inclusion, and environmental protection. However, the implementation of these goals is frequently stymied by "short-termism" -the tendency of governments and corporations to prioritize immediate financial or political gains over long-term ecological health. This is particularly evident in the exploitation of fossil fuels, where the immediate energy needs of a growing population are met at the expense of climate stability. To transition toward a truly green economy, experts argue that a systemic shift is required, moving away from Gross Domestic Product (GDP) as the sole measure of success and instead incorporating "Natural Capital" into national accounting. This would involve valuing forests, clean water, and biodiversity as tangible assets, rather than inexhaustible free resources. Question: What is the author’s tone regarding the current use of GDP as the sole measure of success?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- Critical. 📌 Explanation: The author describes GDP as the "sole measure" that needs to be moved away from because it fails to account for environmental health, implying the current system is inadequate. Passage Insight: Tone Analysis: Whenever an author suggests a "systemic shift" is required because a current method "stymies" progress or overlooks "tangible assets," their tone is critical of the status quo. Why other options are incorrect: (A) Enthusiastic: The author is not praising the current use of GDP. (B) Neutral: The author takes a clear stand that the current system is failing to meet sustainable goals. (D) Indifferent: The author cares enough to propose a specific alternative (Natural Capital)

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Corroborate. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Glamour - खूबसूरती - attractiveness and elegance Example: The movie star's glamour charmed everyone. Corroborate - पुष्टि करना - to support or confirm Example: The witness corroborated the police's statement. Federal - संघीय - related to a central government Example: The federal government is responsible for national defense. Defunct - मृत - no longer existing or working Example: The company is now defunct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The article expounds the benefits of meditation and mindfulness in daily life.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Delineate Let’s see the meaning of all words: Expound - विस्तार से बताना - to explain in detail Example: The professor expounded on the theory. Cheerful - खुश - happy Example: She was cheerful despite the rainy day. Banish - निर्वासित करना - to send away Example: The king banished the traitor. Extreme - चरम - very severe Example: The extreme weather caused chaos. Delineate - वर्णन करना - to describe or explain Example: The report delineates the company's financial status.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Ecstatic
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Miserable Let’s see the meaning of all words: Ecstatic - अत्यधिक खुश - extremely happy Example: She was ecstatic about her birthday party. Flair - स्वभाव - a natural talent or style Example: She has a flair for design. Laggard - पिछड़ा हुआ - slow or late Example: The company was a laggard in adopting new tech. Miserable - दुखी - very unhappy Example: He felt miserable after the breakup. Slender - पतला - thin or narrow Example: The slender tree swayed in the wind.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Blow one’s own trumpet
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - To boast about their achievements Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Blow one’s own trumpet - अपने बारे में बढ़ा-चढ़ा कर बताना - to boast about one's achievements Example: He's always blowing his own trumpet about his awards.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Drizzle
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Trickle Let’s see the meaning of all words: Drizzle - बूंदाबांदी - light rain Example: It's starting to drizzle outside. Trickle - धीरे-धीरे गिरना - to flow or fall slowly Example: Water started to trickle from the tap. Ceased - बंद कर दिया - stopped Example: The company ceased operations last year. Tutelage - संरक्षण - guidance or supervision Example: The student was under his tutelage for years. Moronic - मूर्खतापूर्ण - very foolish Example: That was a moronic thing to do.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The crowd mocked the celebrity's effigy at the carnival.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – original Let’s see the meaning of all words: Effigy - पुतला - a representation of a person Example: The crowd mocked the celebrity's effigy at the carnival. Eyed - देखा - looked at Example: She eyed the suspicious package warily. Brace - तैयार करना - to prepare Example: The team braced themselves for the big match. Original - असली - the real thing Example: The celebrity attended the event in person. Apostolic - धर्मप्रचारक - related to the Pope Example: The Pope's apostolic visit was highly anticipated.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A rabbit’s dwelling
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Burrow Let’s see the meaning of all words: Peter - पीटर - a name, a male given name Example: Peter is coming over tonight. Chore - काम - a task or job, often routine or unpleasant Example: Doing laundry is a weekly chore. Glutton - पेटू - a person who eats excessively or greedily Example: He's a glutton for pizza. Burrow - बिल - a hole or tunnel dug by an animal for shelter or habitation Example: Rabbits often live in burrows.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. My aunt keeps an open house on Sundays, so we can drop by anytime.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Welcomes all members Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Keeps an open house - सबके लिए घर खुला रखना - welcomes guests at any time Example: The new café keeps an open house on Fridays, so customers can come and enjoy live music.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. The act of looking back on past time
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Retrospection Let’s see the meaning of all words: Truant - अनुपस्थित - a student who stays away from school without permission Example: The teacher marked him as a truant. Wreath - माला - a circular arrangement of flowers or leaves Example: She wore a beautiful wreath on her head. Retrospection - आत्म-निरीक्षण - the act of looking back on past events or experiences Example: In retrospection, I realized I made a big mistake. Barrel - बैरल - a large container for liquids or goods Example: The wine is aged in oak barrels.
